name: Yemista Me Lahano (Stuffed Cabbage Leaves)
recipeInstructions:
Serves: 6 Cooking time: 1 1/2 hours
1. Gently fry onion in oil until soft.
2. Mix the cooked onion into the meat with rice, tomato, herbs, and cinnamon. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Divide the mixture into 24 portions.
4. Blanch cabbage leaves in boiling, salted water for 5 minutes until softened. Drain and cut out the thick center of larger leaves (very large leaves may be cut in half).
5. Place one portion of stuffing on the base of a leaf, turn up the base, fold in the sides, and wrap firmly into a neat roll. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
6. Place the rolls close together, seam sides down, in a deep pan lined with cabbage trimmings. Add stock or water, butter, salt, and pepper to taste. Invert a heavy plate on top of the rolls and cover the pan tightly.
7. Simmer gently for 1 1/2 hours.
8. When cooked, carefully drain off the stock into a small saucepan. Reduce the remaining liquid to about 1 1/2 cups over heat and thicken with cornflour mixed with a little cold water. Let it boil for 1 minute.
9. Beat egg whites in a bowl until stiff, then add yolks and beat thoroughly. Gradually add lemon juice and boiling stock. Return the sauce to the small pan, place over low heat, and stir constantly until the egg is cooked (do not boil).
10. Arrange rolls on a heated serving dish and spoon some of the sauce over them. Garnish with chopped dill or parsley and serve the remaining sauce separately. Serve with mashed potatoes.
Note: Grape vine leaves may be used instead of cabbage - about 40 will be required since they take less filling.
From: "The Complete Middle East Cookbook" by Tess Mallory.
